stories.title: End-to-end photo upload slice
stories.description:
Personal Flask + SQLite web app to log treadmill runs from a phone photo and track trends.

This first ticket delivers the **end-to-end upload slice** only. Explicitly **out of scope** here (they become follow-up issues GH-2+): the dashboard with distance bar chart + pace/duration trends, the photo timeline view, and edit/delete of existing entries.

## Context
- Single user, no auth (first cut).
- The user photographs the treadmill's run-summary console (sometimes with themselves in frame, often not perfectly clear).
- Run stats are extracted from that photo via the **Claude vision API** (default model `claude-sonnet-4-6`, configurable). The app needs `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` at runtime.
- Date/time come from the photo's EXIF `DateTimeOriginal` (NOT the upload time); fall back to upload time and flag the entry if EXIF is absent.
- Pace is derived from distance/duration when the console only shows speed. Calories/incline captured if present, optional.
acceptance_criteria: Flask app skeleton with a SQLite database and a home page that renders without error.
SQLite schema for a run entry: photo path, distance, duration, pace, run datetime (from EXIF), `note` (how the run felt), optional calories/incline, and a flag for "EXIF date missing".
Mobile-friendly upload form (`<input type="file" accept="image/*">`) that saves the uploaded photo to local disk.
On upload, the app calls Claude vision to extract distance, duration, and pace from the photo, and reads EXIF `DateTimeOriginal` for the run datetime.
A confirmation page shows the extracted values pre-filled and editable, so the user can correct anything before saving (covers the "extraction unclear -> user inputs" case).
On confirm, the entry is persisted to SQLite and the photo is retained on disk.
The Claude vision call is isolated behind a small function that is stubbed/mocked in tests, so `pytest` passes in CI without a live API key or network.
`uv run pytest -q` is green.

stories.title: Edit and delete saved run entries
stories.description:
Follow-up to GH-1. Adds **edit and delete** for existing run entries, so the user can correct a bad vision extraction after saving or remove a mistaken upload.

## Context
- Single user, no auth.
- Run entries live in the `runs` table (GH-1); photos live under `instance/uploads/`.
- Editable fields mirror the GH-1 confirm page: distance, duration, pace, run_at, note, calories, incline.
acceptance_criteria: From the dashboard (or a run-detail view), each run has **Edit** and **Delete** actions.
Edit opens a form pre-filled with the current values (reusing the confirm-page field layout) and `POST`s an update that persists to SQLite.
Delete removes the `runs` row and the associated photo file from disk, after a confirmation step.
Delete is guarded against removing files outside the upload folder (operate only on the row's stored photo path).
Editing or deleting a non-existent run id returns a clean 404, not a 500.
Tests cover: an edit updates the row, and a delete removes both the row and the file — using the existing SQLite test fixture.
`uv run pytest -q` is green.

stories.title: Photo timeline view of post-run photos
stories.description:
Follow-up to GH-1. Adds a **photo timeline** so the user can see their post-run photos over time (the visual-progress half of the app), complementing the numeric dashboard (separate ticket).

## Context
- Single user, no auth.
- Photos are already saved on disk by GH-1 (`instance/uploads/<uuid>_<name>`) and their path + run datetime are in the `runs` table.
- Run datetime is the EXIF capture date (fallback upload time, flagged) — sort by that, not insertion order.
acceptance_criteria: A `/timeline` page (linked from home/dashboard) shows each run's photo as a thumbnail in a date-ordered timeline, most recent first.
Each timeline item shows the run date and key stats (distance, pace) beneath/over the thumbnail.
Clicking a thumbnail opens the full-size photo.
Photos are served safely from the upload folder (no path traversal; only files referenced by a `runs` row).
Empty state (no photos yet) renders cleanly.
A test asserts `/timeline` returns 200 and references a known saved photo, using the existing SQLite test fixture.
`uv run pytest -q` is green.

stories.title: Dashboard: list runs + distance bar chart + pace/duration trends
stories.description:
Follow-up to GH-1. Adds the **dashboard** so logged runs are actually viewable and trends are visible. Builds on the existing Flask + SQLite app (`runs` table) and the home/upload/confirm/save flow.

## Context
- Single user, no auth (consistent with GH-1).
- Trends only — **no goals / no "missed days"** (the user opted for trends over targets).
- Data already captured per run (GH-1 `runs` table): distance, duration, pace, run_at (EXIF datetime), note, optional calories/incline, exif_date_missing.
- Charting should stay lightweight (e.g. a small JS lib like Chart.js via CDN, or server-rendered SVG) — no heavy frontend build.
acceptance_criteria: A `/dashboard` page (linked from the home page) lists all saved runs, most recent first, showing date, distance, duration, pace, and note.
A **bar chart of distance over time** (x = run date, y = distance), so progress is visible at a glance.
**Trend lines/series for pace and duration over time**, so the user can see how their runs have evolved.
Charts render from real `runs` rows (no hardcoded data) and handle the empty state (no runs yet) gracefully.
Runs flagged `exif_date_missing = 1` are still plotted (using the fallback datetime) but visually marked as estimated.
A test asserts `/dashboard` returns 200 and includes the chart container + a known run's values, using the existing SQLite test fixture.
`uv run pytest -q` is green.
